heading: Use of aircraft for photographing defense installations
label: 796 Use of aircraft for photographing defense installations
text: Whoever uses or permits the use of an aircraft or any contrivance used, or designed for navigation or flight in the air, for the purpose of making a photograph, sketch, picture, drawing, map, or graphical representation of vital military or naval installations or equipment, in violation of , shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.section 795 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on sections 45, 45a, and 45c of title 50, U.S.C., 1940 ed., War and National Defense (, §§ 1, 2, 4, , 4).Jan. 12, 1938, ch. 252 Stat. 3Reference to persons causing or procuring was omitted as unnecessary in view of definition of “principal” in .section 2 of this titlePunishment provided by is repeated, and is from said , U.S.C., 1940 ed.section 795 of this titlesection 45 of title 50Minor changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $1,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
